title: Deterministic shared-Worker timeout capture test (onboard suite) for slow runners
action: CI verify(22) on 2-core runner: skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs 'shared Worker timeout preserves captured stdout and stderr' FAILs because the hanging-module case uses timeout:100 — the child can be SIGTERMed before node even boots and prints, so captured output is legitimately empty. Fix the CLASS per rel1710rc-3 discipline (structure over scheduler timing): split the two properties — (1) timeout semantics (null status, SIGTERM, ETIMEDOUT) may keep a short timeout but must NOT assert output; (2) capture-preservation-on-timeout uses a prints-then-hangs module with a GENEROUS timeout (>=5000ms) so the kill always lands after the child's first-millisecond prints. Sweep the rest of test_onboard_bee.mjs for other short-timeout output assertions. Prove: taskset -c 0,1 node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs 10/10 green, full run_verify green.

truths: timeout semantics checks still assert null/SIGTERM/ETIMEDOUT
prohibitions: Do not delete the capture-preservation assertion
Do not weaken SIGTERM/ETIMEDOUT semantics checks

trace.verify_output: 10/10 runs: PASS - failures: 0, skipped: 1 (each). Full verify: PASS run_verify: 54 suite(s), concurrency=5, wall=57157ms (all 54 suites PASS, including test_guards.mjs 31 passed 0 failed and test_onboard_bee.mjs).
